---
title: "Session 1: Getting Started with AI Tools"
subtitle: "April 22, 2026 — AI Bootcamp for UVM Economics"
---

## April 22, 11:00--12:30, Old Mill A500

This is a watch-along session. No installation needed — we demo everything live. Slides will be visible during AI processing time so you can follow the concepts while the tools work.

### Slides

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Getting Started (Beam & Aslim) | [HTML](slides/s1-intro.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s1-intro.pdf) |
| Terminal + Claude Code (Beam) | [HTML](slides/s1-emily.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s1-emily.pdf) |
| Desktop AI + Teaching Applications (Aslim) | [HTML](slides/s1-erkmen.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s1-erkmen.pdf) |

### What we'll cover

- What AI tools exist and which ones are useful for academic work
- The difference between chat-based tools and agentic tools that work directly on your files
- Live demos: terminal workflows, desktop AI, and a teaching application
- What to expect, what to be skeptical about, and where to start

### Before next time (April 27)

- [ ] Try using an AI tool for one real task this week
- [ ] Install the tools from the [prep guide](prep.qmd) if you haven't already
- [ ] Think of a research or teaching task you'd like to automate

### Resources

- [Getting Set Up](prep.qmd) — installation and cost guide
- [Outside Resources](resources.qmd) — tools, guides, and examples
- [Claude Code docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- [UVM Microsoft Copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot) (free for all UVM)
